narration: Course Schedule is a directed graph problem because one class points to the class that depends on it.
A cycle means the classes are stuck waiting on one another, so no starting point exists inside that loop.
decision: A -> B -> C -> A is impossible because the loop never gives you a first class.

predictionPrompt.prompt: What does a cycle mean in Course Schedule?

choices.label: The schedule is impossible
choices.feedback: Correct. The loop blocks every class in that cycle.

choices.label: The schedule finishes faster
choices.feedback: Not this one. A cycle causes a dependency problem, not a speed boost.

narration: Directed edges and cycle detection are the two biggest clues in this kind of dependency problem.
callout: A clean one-way chain is fine. The trouble starts when a path loops back.
